forecast-period.text: A high pressure system south of the Bight extends a ridge over SA. The high will move east during today and Saturday ahead of a weakening trough that is expected to brush the west and south on the weekend. The high is forecast to be over the Tasman Sea by Monday, extending a ridge over South Australia through until Tuesday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
South to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots north of Cowell to Wallaroo during the afternoon and evening. Winds tending east to southeasterly in the late afternoon.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres north of Cowell to Wallaroo during the afternoon.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Easterly 10 to 15 knots turning northeasterly in the early morning. Winds reaching up to 20 knots south of Port Neill to Port Victoria in the late evening.
South to southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to around 1 metre around midday.
Northerly 15 to 20 knots shifting south to southwesterly during the day.
The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning. Mostly sunny afternoon.
